Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper states SgHCT-F is GenBank accession PQ336776 in Results and Accession Numbers.

Source_Species.reason: All characterized sequences are from Sarcandra glabra.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species throughout the paper is Sarcandra glabra.

Applied_Species.reason: The gene was studied in Sarcandra glabra tissues and sequence source is S. glabra.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: SgHCT-F is one of five BAHD acyltransferase sequences; phylogenetically placed in clade 6c; groups with BBTs; motif altered from HxxxDG to HTISDA/HxxxDA.

Omics_Data.reason: Candidates were identified from the 1kP transcriptome, sample code OSHQ, via BLASTP.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: A benzoyl-ester/benzyl benzoate-like role is only hypothesized; no metabolite product was demonstrated for SgHCT-F.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper explicitly states no donor or acceptor substrates and no benzyl benzoate formation were identified for SgHCT-F, so a terminal metabolite cannot be assigned.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper reports cDNA amplification, heterologous expression in E. coli, protein purification, substrate screening, phylogenetic analysis, and tissue expression analysis for SgHCT-F.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Figure 4e and text state no relative expression in young leaves, with expression in other tissues and highest in old stems; Discussion also notes highest levels in young roots and old stems.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Plant material used was Sarcandra glabra cuttings IPEN-No. JP-0-DUSS-4034.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Same plant material accession IPEN-No. JP-0-DUSS-4034 was used.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points match the paper: identified as BAHD candidate, expressed in E. coli with unresolved substrates, and phylogenetically grouped with BBT-like enzymes suggesting benzoyl ester involvement.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states a 91 bp intron, >75% similarity to predicted benzoyl-CoA:benzyl alcohol O-benzoyltransferases, and failure to observe benzyl benzoate formation.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Expression was detected in all tested tissues except young leaves; text and Figure 4e support flowers, old leaves, young stems, old stems, young roots, and old roots.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were grown under ambient light conditions and expression was analyzed across tissues.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an application-oriented extrapolation not stated or tested in the paper.

corrections.reason: The paper explicitly states no donor or acceptor substrates and no benzyl benzoate formation were identified for SgHCT-F, so a terminal metabolite cannot be assigned.

corrections.old_value: Currently limited because catalytic function is unresolved; may be relevant for future engineering of benzoyl ester metabolism once substrates are identified.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper gives SgHST as GenBank PP449349.

Source_Species.reason: SgHST was isolated from Sarcandra glabra.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Plant material accession IPEN-No. JP-0-DUSS-4034 is provided for the S. glabra plants used.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper names SgHST a hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA:shikimic acid hydroxycinnamoyltransferase.

EC_Number.reason: The Results section gives E.C. number 2.3.1.133 for SgHST.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: SgHST is a BAHD acyltransferase, and the conserved HxxxDG and DFGWG motifs are present in all sequences except noted exceptions not including SgHST.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The work concerns phenolic/phenylpropanoid metabolism and hydroxycinnamate ester biosynthesis; SgHST forms hydroxycinnamoylshikimate esters within this pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: SgHST mainly forms shikimic acid esters and also quinic acid esters at lower efficiency, matching this branch description.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The Discussion describes shikimate esters as transit products/precursors, supporting a precursor-forming midstream step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Results and Table 1 show preferred donors p-coumaroyl-CoA and caffeoyl-CoA with shikimic acid as main acceptor.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper states SgHST mainly forms p-coumaroyl- and caffeoylshikimic acid, specifically 5-O esters.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This matches the Results description: transfer mainly from p-coumaroyl-/caffeoyl-CoA to shikimic acid 5-OH, with lower activity toward quinic and hydroxyanthranilic acids.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: SgHST products are described as transit products/precursors rather than final major end products.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish rate limitation, but does state SgHST is the by far most effective BAHD/highest catalytic efficiency among those characterized.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Caffeoyl-5-O-shikimic acid is detected in plant tissues and directly produced by SgHST.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Hydroxycinnamoylshikimic acids are phenylpropanoid derivatives/hydroxycinnamate esters.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper states caffeoyl-5-O-shikimic acid was highest in young and old stems.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Consistent with biochemical data and Discussion that SgHST promotes formation of p-coumaroyl-/caffeoylshikimic acids, mainly p-coumaroyl-5-O-shikimic acid.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 4a/text: highest in young stems and young roots; lower in old stems and old roots; not expressed in flowers or leaves.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same as above, highest in young stems and young roots.

Omics_Data.reason: Sequence identified from 1kP transcriptome OSHQ; metabolites profiled by HPLC/LC-MS.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were grown under ambient light and analyzed by tissue context.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported: heterologous expression, purified enzyme assays, LC-MS/HPLC, kinetics, and tissue expression analysis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements are directly supported by the Results and Discussion.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Substrate promiscuity and in vitro amide formation are supported, but the statement about causing off-pathway products in engineering contexts is speculative and not tested.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states high affinity for 3-hydroxyanthranilic acid in vitro, absence of corresponding metabolites in planta, and inference that p-coumaroyl-5-O-shikimic acid is primary in vivo product.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Engineering utility is not evaluated in the paper; this is extrapolative.

corrections.old_value: Useful for engineering caffeoyl/p-coumaroyl shikimate pools and related phenylpropanoid derivatives; substrate promiscuity should be considered in pathway design.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Because SgHQT1 directly forms chlorogenic acid, a direct product-forming midstream/downstream step is supported.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: SgHQT1 directly forms chlorogenic acid, a detected final product.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish rate-limiting control.
